title: Recall must not run full duplicate quality checks per packet
summary: The synthetic memory scale benchmark exposed a recall latency bug: packets without stored quality.score made recall call evaluateMemoryQuality for every scored packet, which triggered duplicate scans during query time. R
body: The synthetic memory scale benchmark exposed a recall latency bug: packets without stored quality.score made recall call evaluateMemoryQuality for every scored packet, which triggered duplicate scans during query time. Recall now uses a lightweight quality fallback and keeps expensive duplicate detection in validation/review reports. A 5,000-packet scale run now indexes in 7.8s and recalls with 334ms median latency at 100% hit rate @10.
